meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't collaborate (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't collaborate. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Solve Collaboration Issues
content.seo_description: Overcome collaboration bottlenecks with the Antigravity project template. A beginner-friendly guide to building scalable apps using Claude Code and AI tools.
content.answer_block: Collaboration friction kills projects. The Antigravity project template standardizes your repository structure, enabling seamless AI-assisted development. By integrating Claude Code, you transform individual coding efforts into a collaborative, modular workflow. This checklist guides you through setting up this architecture to ensure your code is always production-ready and team-accessible.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen of spaghetti code, feeling the crushing weight of a project that refuses to scale. You want to build, but the lack of structure turns every feature request into a catastrophic merge conflict. You aren't just hitting a wall; you are experiencing the primary bottleneck of modern software development: the inability to sync intent with execution.

The relief you seek isn't a better IDE—it's a better foundation. The Antigravity project template provides the guardrails necessary to turn your chaotic repo into a structured ecosystem. By pairing this with Claude Code, you bridge the gap between architectural vision and working code, allowing you to build with the precision of a senior engineer from day one.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Establish the root directory structure to ensure all future AI interactions follow a standardized, predictable schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize an Antigravity project structure with modular architecture, separating core logic, API interfaces, and state management.

steps.title: Define the Collaboration Schema
steps.description: Create a documentation layer that acts as the 'source of truth' for the AI to reference when making changes across multiple files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project-context-map.md that outlines the data flow between components to prevent cross-module conflicts.

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Access
steps.description: Grant Claude Code restricted access to your workspace to automate testing and documentation updates without manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the .claudeignore and security policies to protect sensitive keys while enabling full repo analysis.

steps.title: Implement Modular Feature Branches
steps.description: Use the template's isolation patterns to build features that don't collide with existing codebase logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new feature module for user authentication following the Antigravity dependency injection pattern.

steps.title: Validate Integrity with AI-Driven Tests
steps.description: Automate the verification of your code to ensure that every 'collaborative' change maintains system stability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of integration tests that validate the interaction between the auth module and the database layer.

pro_tips: Always prepend your Claude Code prompts with '--context-file' to ensure it understands the specific Antigravity architectural constraints.
Treat your README.md as a living API contract; if it isn't updated in the prompt, the AI will eventually drift from your architecture.
Use 'claude chat' to perform 'code reviews' on your own PRs before merging to catch structural inconsistencies early.
Keep your 'context-map' files under 500 tokens to ensure the AI maintains high-fidelity memory of your project structure.
Leverage the 'antigravity-hooks' to auto-format your code; clean code is the best way to ensure successful AI-human collaboration.
resources: Best Practices for AI Engineering: https://ai.engineering/handbook
content.next_steps: Once your project is initialized, deploy your MVP to a staging environment and run a 'claude audit' to identify potential security gaps before your first real-world user interaction.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to build faster and cleaner than 99% of developers. Stop struggling with disorganized repos and start shipping features that actually scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
